important_rule: This crosswalk normalizes and reconciles denominator/grain only; it is NOT external validation evidence and defines NO pass/fail bands. Failure-unit IDs, value shares, hazard axis, curve form, and gust-demand->DR ordinates are taken verbatim from the canonical strong_wind_solar cell (model_v1_0). It is a source-pathway / normalization layer, NOT a calibration harness.
scope_note: This pair (solar_strong_wind) covers utility-scale ground-mount PV under STRONG / STRAIGHT-LINE CONVECTIVE wind (synoptic gusts, derecho, microburst, gust-front outflow). TORNADO and HURRICANE/TC are fenced OUT (quarantined) — the canonical strong_wind_solar cell is a straight-line-wind demand curve and does not carry tornado vortex or TC-duration loading. The v1.0 cell uses a single thresholded-logistic demand curve per failure unit on a 3-second-gust-at-array-height axis.

hazard_axis.unit: dimensionless effective demand ratio (input gust in mph)
hazard_axis.design_note: V_design is the ASCE 7-22 §29.4.5 site design 3s gust for the ground-mount array; mult carries directionality/gust-front amplification.

strong_wind_damage_grain.curve_form: thresholded_logistic_demand  (DR = max_DR / (1 + exp(-k*(R_eff - R50))) for R_eff >= R0, else 0)
strong_wind_damage_grain.f_kind: material/structural capacity vs effective gust-demand ratio (NOT geometry/elevation, NOT BOM/material share)
strong_wind_damage_grain.owns: severity curves only — EAL / PML / VaR / TVaR are downstream (hazard catalog + financial model), NOT in this cell

failure_units.failure_unit: Single-axis tracker structural failure (torque tube / bearing / drive)

derived_bucket_shares.note: primary_structural_aggregate = TRACKER_STRUCT + RACKING_STRUCT + MODULE_ATTACH + FOUNDATION_UPLIFT (the four include_in_default_structural_aggregate=true units). SCADA_EXPOSED is a secondary open-seam and is EXCLUDED from the default aggregate. NOTE the two mounting FUs (tracker vs racking) are ALTERNATIVE architectures on a given site, not additive — a plant is either tracker OR fixed-tilt; summing both overstates a single-architecture plant.

comparison_guidance: A whole-plant modeled event loss (e.g. AXIS failed-stow $380,000/MW) CANNOT be placed cleanly on a per-failure-unit DR axis, because (a) it is HAIL-stow framed rather than isolated straight-line wind, and (b) it blends module + mounting + downtime. Only per-subsystem or clearly module-attach / mounting-isolated figures are grain-comparable to these DR curves. A $/MW exceeding a failure-unit hardware cap (e.g. FEMP $1.5M/MW top tier > installed TIV) is a GRAIN WARNING that repair+debris+replace can exceed asset TIV, NOT evidence DR>1. Straight-line convective wind $ is not cleanly isolated in ANY public source, so every crosswalk verdict here is grain-qualified.
